scripts.// tiering: eval:smoke (per-PR, non-gating) and eval:full (main/release, deploy-blocking) auto-discover every *-oq9.spec.ts under __tests__ (Filipino + English + future languages) with zero per-language CI wiring (ADR-0025 §6 / ADR-0026 AC #5). SCOPE NOTE (code-review patch P7): the positional `src/__tests__` is a vitest path FILTER (matched as a prefix over resolved test-file paths), NOT a shell glob — vitest does not expand glob positional args, so `src/__tests__/**/*.spec.ts` matches nothing. The filter therefore runs every file under __tests__ that the vitest config `include` matches (`src/**/*.spec.ts` + `src/**/*.test.ts`). Today only `*-oq9.spec.ts` files live there; a future `.test.ts` under __tests__ WOULD be picked up by this filter. Keep gate specs as `*.spec.ts` and put helper tests elsewhere (or tighten the vitest `include` for the eval package) if that becomes load-bearing. The smoke/full distinction is enforced by Turbo/CI wiring (eval:full task pinned cache:false so releases re-execute from source; eval:full runs only on main/release per .github/workflows/eval-full.yml), NOT by a package-level env var — the prior IIP_EVAL_FULL was a no-op (read by nothing) and has been removed. NOTE (Story 2.6c): the prior scripts hardcoded the Filipino path, which silently dropped every other language spec — the glob fix makes AC #5's 'auto-discovered with zero edits' claim literally true.
